title: Diagnosing unnecessary re-renders with React DevTools Profiler

performance_problem.symptom: List of 200 items lags on every keystroke in unrelated search box
performance_problem.business_impact: Users report typing feels broken; bounce rate +12%

diagnosis_steps: Open React DevTools Profiler, record session
Inspect commit phase; see all 200 ListItem renders
Identify: cause is parent context value reference instability
anti_patterns_to_avoid: React.memo on every leaf component (still re-renders when context changes)
useMemo with new object on every render
Splitting components without addressing root cause
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Profile first; the bottleneck is rarely where you guess.
2. Context value object changing reference = all consumers re-render even if values equal.
3. Two fixes: useMemo the context value (preferred for single context) OR split context (state vs dispatch — see test-023).
4. React.memo at leaf is useless if props (or context) change reference each render.
5. Measure after fix; budget regression detection.
trade_offs: Context split = more boilerplate; useMemo = subtle dep array bugs
regression_prevention: Render count assertion in tests (test-023); INP budget in Lighthouse CI
what_breaks_naive_approach: React.memo + new context value: still all re-render; perf untouched.

title: Identifying and removing duplicate npm dependencies

performance_problem.symptom: Bundle 2.4MB after compression; competitor at 600KB
performance_problem.metric_impact: LCP 4.2s on 3G; initial JS download dominates
performance_problem.business_impact: Mobile conversion rate 30% lower than desktop

diagnosis_steps: Identify duplicate packages (different versions in node_modules)
pnpm why X — find who pulls each version
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Adding deps without bundle impact review
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Duplicates from semver: lib A wants react@17, lib B wants react@18 → 2 copies.
2. Visualizer reveals; pnpm why traces.
3. Resolutions override pinning.
4. Side-effects:false in package.json enables tree-shaking.
5. Per-feature imports: `import {map} from 'lodash-es'` not `import _ from 'lodash'`.
trade_offs: Resolutions may break libs that depend on specific versions; test thoroughly
regression_prevention: CI bundle-size check; visualizer in dev
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tree-shaking with CommonJS deps: bundlers can't eliminate; whole lib shipped.

title: Reducing request waterfall with HTTP/2 + parallel fetching

performance_problem.symptom: Page loads in 6.2s; waterfall shows sequential blocking requests

diagnosis_steps: Chrome DevTools Network tab; record from cache cleared
Identify waterfall (each request waits for prior)
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. HTTP/2 multiplexes; ensure enabled on origin + CDN.
2. Promise.all for independent requests.
3. Preconnect / dns-prefetch hints in <head>.
4. Critical path: identify the longest chain, parallelize what's not on it.
5. Defer non-critical JS via defer or async attributes.
trade_offs: Parallel = more concurrent server load; ensure server scales
regression_prevention: Lighthouse CI tracks LCP; alert on regression
what_breaks_naive_approach: Sequential async/await chains hide that requests could run together.

title: N+1 query detection and elimination

performance_problem.symptom: GET /orders endpoint times out for users with 200+ orders

diagnosis_steps: Find query repeated N times per request
Profile a slow request locally with DEBUG=true
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Caching as Band-Aid (still N+1 on miss)
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. N+1 = canonical perf bug.
2. Eager load via JOIN or batch fetch via DataLoader.
3. ORMs hide this; use raw queries + explicit joins.
4. Detect via query logging in dev + pg_stat_statements counts in prod.
5. Lint rule: no DB calls inside loops.
trade_offs: JOIN can return many rows; for huge children, batch fetch is leaner
regression_prevention: Slow query log alerting; integration test asserts <5 queries per endpoint
what_breaks_naive_approach: ORM lazy loading produces queries silently; profiling reveals catastrophic N+1.

title: Fixing CLS from late-loading hero image

performance_problem.symptom: Page jumps as hero image loads
performance_problem.business_impact: User clicks on wrong button; conversion -8%

diagnosis_steps: Check that width/height attributes are missing
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Async image load without reserved space
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Browser reserves space when img has width + height (uses as aspect ratio).
2. CSS aspect-ratio property does the same for arbitrary boxes.
3. Modern: just set width+height attributes — they're treated as ratio in CSS layout.
4. Above-fold: loading='eager' + fetchpriority='high'.
5. Below-fold: loading='lazy'.
trade_offs: Must know image dimensions ahead of time; for user-uploaded, use aspect-ratio CSS
regression_prevention: Lighthouse CI threshold CLS < 0.1; visual regression catches layout
what_breaks_naive_approach: No dimensions = no reserved space; image loads in = everything below shifts.

title: Detecting React subscription leaks

performance_problem.symptom: App memory grows 50MB/hour during normal use
performance_problem.metric_impact: After 4h: 200MB+ heap; tab crashes on low-RAM devices

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. React effects need cleanup return for subscriptions/timers/listeners.
2. Memory snapshot comparison reveals retained class names.
3. Closures over large objects retain them.
4. WeakRef / WeakMap for caches that shouldn't prevent GC.
5. Strict Mode in dev re-runs effects, catches missing cleanups.
trade_offs: More boilerplate; ESLint react-hooks/exhaustive-deps helps catch
regression_prevention: Memory tests in E2E; alert on growth in RUM
what_breaks_naive_approach: Missing cleanup: subscribers accumulate; memory grows; eventually OOM.

title: Detecting event loop blocking

performance_problem.symptom: Random request timeouts; not correlated with load
performance_problem.metric_impact: event loop lag spikes to 5s during sync operation

diagnosis_steps: JSON.parse on huge payload, sync crypto, regex catastrophe
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Sync APIs in request path (fs.readFileSync, crypto.pbkdf2Sync)
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Event loop is single-threaded; one slow op blocks all.
2. Move CPU-heavy to worker_threads.
3. Stream large parses.
4. Test regex with ReDoS detection.
5. perf_hooks.monitorEventLoopDelay for live monitoring.
trade_offs: Worker_threads have startup cost; pool workers
regression_prevention: Event loop metric alert; load tests
what_breaks_naive_approach: Sync APIs feel fast at low volume; under load they block, requests pile up.

title: Proper useMemo / useCallback usage

performance_problem.symptom: Memoized child still re-renders despite stable props

diagnosis_steps: Inspect why: hover render reason → 'props changed'
Identify which prop is new reference
Track to parent: function passed inline → new each render
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. useMemo/useCallback have cost: deps check + memo storage.
2. Only useful when downstream MEMOIZED component receives them.
3. If child not memo'd, parent memo'ing callback is wasted.
4. Deps array must include all referenced state — eslint-plugin-react-hooks catches.
5. Profile before optimizing.
trade_offs: Memo + useCallback adds maintenance burden; only where measurable
regression_prevention: React Profiler in CI (Lighthouse integration); render counts in tests
what_breaks_naive_approach: useCallback without memoized consumer = wasted; React.memo without stable props = wasted.

title: Dynamic imports for route-based code splitting

performance_problem.symptom: Initial bundle 1.8MB; only home page used
performance_problem.metric_impact: LCP 3.5s; 80% of JS unused on home

diagnosis_steps: Coverage tab in Chrome DevTools shows used vs unused
Each route should be its own chunk
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Loading whole library for one function
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. React.lazy + Suspense splits by component.
2. Router level split is the right grain.
3. Prefetch on hover for likely navigation.
4. preload critical chunks.
5. Vendor chunk separately if shared.
trade_offs: Suspense fallback flashes; mitigate with skeleton
regression_prevention: Bundle size budget per route in CI
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single bundle: users wait for code they never use.

title: Optimizing LCP for hero image with priority hints

performance_problem.symptom: LCP 3.8s on 4G; hero image dominates

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. LCP image MUST be discoverable in HTML; not in JS.
2. fetchpriority='high' tells browser to prioritize.
3. preload in <head> moves request earlier.
4. Right-sized image (responsive srcset).
5. AVIF/WebP for smaller files.
solution: // next.js Image component handles much of this // import Image from 'next/image'; //
trade_offs: Larger HTML; preload uses bandwidth even if not used
regression_prevention: Lighthouse CI LCP budget; visual regression
what_breaks_naive_approach: Lazy-load LCP image: browser delays load → LCP terrible.

title: Using EXPLAIN ANALYZE to fix slow queries

performance_problem.symptom: Order list query takes 2s on production data

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows actual plan, timing.
2. Seq scan on big table = missing or unused index.
3. Estimated rows wildly off actual = stats stale (ANALYZE).
4. BUFFERS shows IO.
5. Index for WHERE + ORDER BY combinations.
solution:
-- before
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 'abc' AND status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;
-- Seq Scan on orders (cost=... actual time=1800ms rows=18 loops=1)
--   Filter: ...
--   Rows Removed by Filter: 9999982

-- add compound index matching WHERE + ORDER BY
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_customer_status_created
ON orders (customer_id, status, created_at DESC);

-- verify
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS)
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 'abc' AND status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 20;
-- Index Scan using idx_orders_... (cost=... actual time=2ms rows=18 loops=1)
trade_offs: Index adds write cost; storage; maintain on bulk insert
regression_prevention: Slow query log alert; query plan stability check
what_breaks_naive_approach: Add index without EXPLAIN: may not be used; misordering kills index utility.

title: Implementing stale-while-revalidate

performance_problem.symptom: Every page nav refetches data; UI flashes

diagnosis_steps: Network tab: same request fires on every nav
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. SWR: serve cached, revalidate in background.
2. SWR/TanStack Query implement this.
3. Cache-Control: max-age=0, stale-while-revalidate=300 → browser handles too.
4. UX: instant content + silent refresh.
trade_offs: Briefly stale data; document staleness
what_breaks_naive_approach: Every page nav refetches; users wait.

title: Virtualizing long lists with TanStack Virtual

performance_problem.symptom: 10k row table freezes on render

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Virtualization renders only visible + overscan.
2. TanStack Virtual handles dynamic heights.
3. Performance: ~30 nodes regardless of total.
4. Scroll handling at 60fps.
trade_offs: Find-in-page doesn't find off-screen; complex layouts harder
what_breaks_naive_approach: Rendering all: page freezes; mobile crashes.

title: Reducing INP by breaking up long tasks

performance_problem.symptom: Click handler causes 800ms freeze

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Long tasks (>50ms) block input.
2. Break with scheduler.yield() (when supported) or setTimeout/queueMicrotask.
3. requestIdleCallback for low-priority.
4. Move heavy to Worker.
trade_offs: Chunked is slower wall-clock; Worker has setup cost
regression_prevention: INP budget in CI; long-task observer in RUM
what_breaks_naive_approach: Long sync task = unresponsive UI.

title: Node.js heap snapshot analysis

performance_problem.symptom: Worker process OOMs after 6 hours
performance_problem.metric_impact: Heap grows from 100MB to 1.5GB; restart triggers downtime

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Heap snapshot reveals retained objects.
2. Comparison surfaces what GREW.
3. Closures over large objects retain entire scope.
4. LRU cache with size bound, not unbounded Map.
5. WeakRef for cache that shouldn't prevent GC.
trade_offs: LRU evictions may cause cache miss; tune size to hit rate
regression_prevention: Heap monitoring; alert on growth pattern
what_breaks_naive_approach: Unbounded Map: leak inevitable.

title: clinic.js flame for CPU profiling

performance_problem.symptom: CPU spikes to 100% on certain requests

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Flame graph: width = time in function (including children).
2. Wide flames at top = candidates.
3. JSON.parse on huge payload, regex backtracking common culprits.
4. Profile with prod-shape load.
solution:
# install
npm i -g clinic

# run
clinic flame -- node app.js
# in another terminal
autocannon -c 10 -d 30 http://localhost:3000/slow
# Ctrl-C

# clinic opens HTML report — see flame graph

# typical fix: replace slow JSON.parse
// BEFORE
const data = JSON.parse(hugeString);

// AFTER: streaming or simdjson
import simdjson from 'simdjson';
const data = simdjson.parse(hugeString); // 2-5x faster on large
trade_offs: simdjson is native dep; install complexity
what_breaks_naive_approach: Guess + optimize: no improvement, time wasted.

title: React concurrent transitions for non-urgent updates

performance_problem.symptom: Typing in filter slows entire app

diagnosis_steps: Profiler shows whole list re-render per keystroke
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. useTransition marks an update as non-urgent.
2. React can interrupt to handle higher-priority (input).
3. useDeferredValue defers a value's downstream renders.
4. Combine with React.memo on list items.
trade_offs: Stale UI briefly visible; opacity feedback helps
what_breaks_naive_approach: Sync filter on every keystroke = unresponsive input.

title: Connection pool tuning

performance_problem.symptom: DB connection exhaustion under spike
performance_problem.metric_impact: DB rejects connections at 100; app throws 500

anti_patterns_to_avoid: No PgBouncer for many app instances
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. App pool * num_replicas <= DB max_connections * 0.8.
2. PgBouncer multiplexes; transaction mode for max throughput.
3. Connection lifecycle: acquire, query, release ASAP.
4. Long transactions hold connections.
trade_offs: Smaller pool = more queueing under load; balance
what_breaks_naive_approach: Pool too large × N instances > DB max.

title: Replacing moment.js for huge bundle savings

performance_problem.symptom: Moment.js with all locales = 200KB

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Moment deprecated; date-fns tree-shakeable.
2. Per-function import: bundle only what's used.
3. Native Intl.DateTimeFormat for many cases.
trade_offs: Format string syntax differs; codemod helps
regression_prevention: Bundle budget; lint rule banning moment
what_breaks_naive_approach: Moment with locales = 200KB.

title: Setting up Real User Monitoring with web-vitals

performance_problem.symptom: Synthetic Lighthouse passes; real users complain

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. web-vitals library captures CLS, INP, LCP from real users.
2. Send to analytics with sampling.
3. p75 = Google's threshold for 'good'.
4. Slice by device, network, geo to find pockets.
before_metrics: Lab only; blind to real users
after_metrics: p75 LCP per device class visible
trade_offs: Cost of metrics ingestion; sample carefully
what_breaks_naive_approach: Lab metrics lie about real-world.

title: HTTP cache strategy for static assets

performance_problem.symptom: Repeat visitors re-download all assets

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Content-hashed assets: cache forever (immutable).
2. HTML: no-cache or short max-age.
3. immutable directive prevents revalidation.
4. Build system generates hashes (Vite, Webpack do).
trade_offs: Wrong long-cache on dynamic = stale; ensure hashing
what_breaks_naive_approach: No cache headers: re-download every visit.

title: Key prop disasters

performance_problem.symptom: List re-orders cause full re-render + input loss

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. React uses key to track element identity.
2. key=index: when list reorders, React mismatches.
3. key={Math.random()}: new key each render = always remount.
4. Use stable, unique id.
trade_offs: Need stable id; generate on creation
what_breaks_naive_approach: Index key + reorder = unmount/remount everything.

title: Font loading optimization

performance_problem.symptom: Text flashes invisible then snaps to font

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. font-display: swap = show fallback then swap.
2. size-adjust + ascent-override match fallback metrics → no CLS.
3. Variable fonts: one file, many weights.
4. Preload critical font.
trade_offs: Slight metric mismatch tolerable; tools generate adjusters
what_breaks_naive_approach: font-display:block: invisible text (FOIT) for 3s.

title: Closure-retained large objects

performance_problem.symptom: Memory grows after closing modal

diagnosis_steps: Trace: event listener still holds closure
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Adding listener to global that captures component data
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Closure retains ALL variables in scope, even unused.
2. Listener on global retains until removed.
3. Component unmount must remove.
4. Move closures out to module scope where possible.
trade_offs: More cleanup boilerplate
what_breaks_naive_approach: Listener never removed: closure retained forever.

title: Partial indexes for selective queries

performance_problem.symptom: Active-orders query slow

diagnosis_steps: Index would be huge if all rows
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Index all rows when only few queried
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Partial index covers only matching rows.
2. Tiny vs full index.
3. Query must match WHERE in plan.
solution:
-- only index active orders
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_orders_active
ON orders (customer_id, created_at DESC)
WHERE status = 'active';

-- query MUST include status='active' to use
EXPLAIN ANALYZE
SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id='x' AND status='active' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 10;
-- Index Scan using idx_orders_active
trade_offs: Query plan must use the WHERE; queries without status='active' use other index
what_breaks_naive_approach: Full index on rarely-matching column = huge waste.

title: Streaming responses for large payloads

performance_problem.symptom: Large CSV export OOMs server

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Stream: write chunks as you generate.
2. Backpressure: respect drain.
3. Node's pipeline handles errors.
trade_offs: More complex; backpressure handling
what_breaks_naive_approach: Buffer everything = OOM.

title: Context split: state vs dispatch

performance_problem.symptom: Single AuthContext causes 100+ re-renders on any state change

diagnosis_steps: Context value object changes per render
anti_patterns_to_avoid: useMemo on context value (helps but doesn't isolate)
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Split: read consumers separate from write consumers.
2. Stable dispatch = useCallback or set state directly.
3. Only state consumers re-render on state change.
solution: // see test-023
trade_offs: Two contexts to maintain
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single context: cascading re-renders.

title: Treeshaking validation

performance_problem.symptom: Lib X 200KB but only one function used

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Per-function import: import {x} from 'lib/x' if lib supports.
2. Lib with sideEffects:false enables.
3. CJS deps = no tree-shaking.
trade_offs: Lib must support deep imports
what_breaks_naive_approach: Barrel import = whole lib.

title: Cancellation of stale requests

performance_problem.symptom: Type-ahead search: late responses overwrite recent

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. AbortController per request; cancel prior on new.
2. Or track latest request id.
3. SWR/TanStack handles this.
trade_offs: Some server work wasted; trade for UX
what_breaks_naive_approach: Without cancellation: race conditions display wrong data.

title: Performance budget enforcement in CI

performance_problem.symptom: Performance regresses over time

reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Bundle budget in CI fails PRs exceeding.
2. Lighthouse CI tracks scores.
3. Per-route bundle limits.
trade_offs: Sometimes blocks legitimate growth; require override approval
what_breaks_naive_approach: No budget = slow drift to slow.
